title: Damaged building exclusion zone and re-entry boundary
summary: A simple perimeter system that keeps residents and helpers away from collapse, glass, fire, electricity, gas, and falling-object zones until qualified inspection.
content:
A simple perimeter system that keeps residents and helpers away from collapse, glass, fire, electricity, gas, and falling-object zones until qualified inspection.

Use the materials as a compact field kit. Work through the steps in order, pause whenever a warning or stop condition applies, and write down the outcome so another person can continue safely.
steps: Observe only from a safe distance and note leaning walls, loose façades, broken glass, smoke, water flow, utility damage, and blocked access.
Set a conservative boundary beyond the likely fall distance and keep emergency access lanes open.
Post one clear message at every approach: no entry, identified hazards, time set, responsible contact, and official service called.
Maintain an entry log only for authorized responders and move the boundary outward if conditions worsen.
warnings: Tape is a warning, not structural protection.
Never allow a quick property-retrieval visit before official clearance.
common_mistakes: Starting Damaged building exclusion zone and re-entry boundary without first checking immediate danger and official instructions.
Failing to record time, location, responsible person, and unresolved risks for Damaged building exclusion zone and re-entry boundary.
alternatives: Use a paper-only version of Damaged building exclusion zone and re-entry boundary when power or connectivity is unavailable.
Assign one trained helper to simplify Damaged building exclusion zone and re-entry boundary when the group is stressed or short-handed.
failure_signs: Conditions become more dangerous while carrying out Damaged building exclusion zone and re-entry boundary.
Critical information cannot be confirmed or the assigned helper cannot continue Damaged building exclusion zone and re-entry boundary safely.
when_not_to_use: Do not continue Damaged building exclusion zone and re-entry boundary when it requires entering an unstable, contaminated, flooded, burning, electrically hazardous, or officially closed area.
Do not improvise medical, electrical, structural, or rescue actions beyond your training while using Damaged building exclusion zone and re-entry boundary.
short_term: ['Within the first operational period, Damaged building exclusion zone and re-entry boundary should produce a dated status, one responsible person, and a safe next action.', 'Repeat the essential check for Damaged building exclusion zone and re-entry boundary whenever conditions, official advice, or available resources change.']
long_term: ['Include lessons from Damaged building exclusion zone and re-entry boundary in the household or community emergency plan and resupply list.', 'Review the records from Damaged building exclusion zone and re-entry boundary after the event and correct gaps before the next emergency.']
if_method_fails: ['If Damaged building exclusion zone and re-entry boundary cannot be completed, move to the safest verified location, notify the responsible contact, and record what remains unresolved.', 'Use official emergency services or qualified specialists when the limits of Damaged building exclusion zone and re-entry boundary are reached.']
environment_notes: ['Adapt Damaged building exclusion zone and re-entry boundary to local terrain, weather, building type, transport access, language, disability needs, and official restrictions.', 'In Greece, use 112 for urgent emergencies and follow Civil Protection or local-authority instructions relevant to Damaged building exclusion zone and re-entry boundary.']
